findings.vulnerability_type: Critical Clickjacking Risk on Sensitive Page
findings.description: Authentication, payment, and account management pages without clickjacking protection are high-value targets for UI redressing attacks that could lead to account takeover or unauthorized transactions.
findings.observation: Critical Clickjacking Risk on Sensitive Page detected: Sensitive page lacks anti-clickjacking protection: https://webdriveruniversity.com/Login-Portal/index.html
findings.proof_of_concept:
🔓 Clickjacking Vulnerability Proof of Concept

⚠️ Security Alert: This page demonstrates that https://webdriveruniversity.com/Login-Portal/index.html is vulnerable to clickjacking attacks. The target website can be embedded in an iframe and users can be tricked into clicking invisible elements.

Vulnerability Details:

  • Target URL: https://webdriveruniversity.com/Login-Portal/index.html
  • Missing Header: X-Frame-Options or Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors
  • Risk: Attackers can hide the target website under deceptive UI elements

Demonstration:

The target website is embedded below. Notice how it can be framed without restrictions:

🎁 WIN A FREE PRIZE! 🎁

Click the button below to claim your reward!

(This button overlays the target website)

Attack Scenario:

An attacker could:

  1. Create a malicious website that embeds https://webdriveruniversity.com/Login-Portal/index.html in an invisible iframe
  2. Trick users into clicking on deceptive elements (like "Play Video" or "Claim Prize")
  3. The click actually targets buttons/links on your website
  4. This can lead to unauthorized actions (changing settings, making purchases, posting content)

Remediation:

  • Add X-Frame-Options: DENY or SAMEORIGIN header
  • Or use Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none' or 'self'
  • Test with X-Frame-Options: ALLOW-FROM (limited browser support)

This proof of concept demonstrates the security weakness. Remove this header protection to fix the vulnerability.
findings.remediation: Implement X-Frame-Options: DENY on all sensitive pages immediately.

⚠️ Security Alert: This page demonstrates that https://webdriveruniversity.com/js/respond.js is vulnerable to clickjacking attacks. The target website can be embedded in an iframe and users can be tricked into clicking invisible elements.

Vulnerability Details:

  • Target URL: https://webdriveruniversity.com/js/respond.js
  • Missing Header: X-Frame-Options or Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors
  • Risk: Attackers can hide the target website under deceptive UI elements

Demonstration:

The target website is embedded below. Notice how it can be framed without restrictions:

🎁 WIN A FREE PRIZE! 🎁

Click the button below to claim your reward!

(This button overlays the target website)

Attack Scenario:

An attacker could:

  1. Create a malicious website that embeds https://webdriveruniversity.com/js/respond.js in an invisible iframe
  2. Trick users into clicking on deceptive elements (like "Play Video" or "Claim Prize")
  3. The click actually targets buttons/links on your website
  4. This can lead to unauthorized actions (changing settings, making purchases, posting content)

Remediation:

  • Add X-Frame-Options: DENY or SAMEORIGIN header
  • Or use Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none' or 'self'
  • Test with X-Frame-Options: ALLOW-FROM (limited browser support)

This proof of concept demonstrates the security weakness. Remove this header protection to fix the vulnerability.
findings.remediation:
Implement anti-clickjacking protection using one of these methods:
1. Add HTTP header: X-Frame-Options: DENY or SAMEORIGIN
2. Add CSP header: Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none' or 'self'
3. For legacy browsers, implement frame-breaking JavaScript as a defense-in-depth measure
